name: BenchLM pricing
description: Model API pricing with linked BenchLM score and value fields when available.

items.creator: Prism ML
items.note: Self-hosted open-weight model. Public hosted pricing varies by provider and infrastructure.

items.creator: Prism ML
items.note: Self-hosted open-weight model. Public hosted pricing varies by provider and infrastructure.

items.creator: Prism ML
items.note: Self-hosted open-weight model. Public hosted pricing varies by provider and infrastructure.

items.creator: Aion Labs
items.note: Aion Labs' official API reference lists Aion-2.0 pricing at 0.0000008 prompt tokens and 0.0000016 completion tokens, equivalent to $0.80 input / $1.60 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's pricing docs list Claude Haiku 3 at $0.25 input / $1.25 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's pricing docs list Claude Opus 3 at $15 input / $75 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's pricing docs list Claude Sonnet 3.5 at $3 input / $15 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's pricing docs list Claude Sonnet 4 at $3 input / $15 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's pricing docs list Claude Opus 4.1 at $15 input / $75 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic documents extended thinking for Opus 4.1, but does not publish a separate priced `claude-4-1-opus-thinking` SKU.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's June 9, 2026 Claude Fable 5 model page says Claude Fable 5 is available through the Claude API as `claude-fable-5` at $10 input / $50 output per million tokens, with a 90% input-token discount for prompt caching. US-only inference is available at 1.1x pricing.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic official public pricing for Claude Haiku 4.5 as referenced by Anthropic's April 2026 model pages.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's June 9, 2026 Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 launch says both models are offered at $10 input / $50 output per million tokens. Mythos 5 is restricted to Glasswing partners and trusted access programs.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.5 launch page lists Opus 4.5 at $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic public pricing updated for the current Opus tier; Anthropic's April 16, 2026 Claude Opus 4.7 announcement says pricing remains the same as Opus 4.6 at $5/$25 per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic official pricing from the Claude Opus 4.7 announcement dated April 16, 2026. Anthropic states pricing remains the same as Opus 4.6: $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic official pricing for Claude Opus 4.7 applies to the adaptive reasoning variant as the same API model with effort controls; launch announcement states pricing remains $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's May 28, 2026 Claude Opus 4.8 launch announcement says Opus 4.8 is available at the same price as Opus 4.7. BenchLM maps that to the current Opus API rate of $5 input / $25 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.6 announcement says pricing remains the same as Sonnet 4.5 at $3 input / $15 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Anthropic
items.note: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 4.6 announcement says pricing remains the same as Sonnet 4.5 at $3 input / $15 output per million tokens.

items.creator: Cohere
items.note: Cohere's current public docs list the `command-a-plus-05-2026` generative model at $2.50 input / $10.00 output per million tokens. Cohere's May 20, 2026 launch post and model card establish the exact Command A+ context as 128K input with 64K max generation; self-hosted or private deployment costs vary by infrastructure.

items.creator: Cursor
items.note: Cursor's official Composer 2 API pricing. Cursor also documents a faster in-product variant at $1.50/$7.50 per million tokens.

items.creator: Cursor
items.note: Cursor's official Composer 2.5 docs list the standard tier at $0.50/$2.50 per million tokens and document a faster in-product variant with the same intelligence at $3/$15 per million tokens.

items.creator: Databricks
items.note: Self-hosted cost varies

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek's current public API pricing pages price `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner`, but do not publish a current standalone token rate for the exact DeepSeek Coder 2.0 SKU. BenchLM treats hosted pricing as unavailable for this exact row.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: Open-weight model. Self-hosted or third-party hosted costs vary by provider and infrastructure.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek's official DeepSeek-R1 release page lists $0.55 input (cache miss) / $2.19 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.14.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: Self-hosted distilled open-weight model. Public hosted pricing varies by provider.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek's official DeepSeek-V3 launch page lists $0.27 input (cache miss) / $1.10 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.07.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: Open-weight model. Self-hosted or third-party hosted costs vary by provider and infrastructure.

items.creator: DeepSeek

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek's current pricing page lists `deepseek-chat` as DeepSeek-V3.2 (non-thinking mode) at $0.28 input (cache miss) / $0.42 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.028.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek's current pricing page lists `deepseek-reasoner` as the hosted DeepSeek-V3.2 thinking-mode endpoint at $0.55 input (cache miss) / $2.19 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.14.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek lists `deepseek-v4-flash` at $0.14 input (cache miss) / $0.28 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.028.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek Table 7 reports this as the High reasoning-effort mode of DeepSeek V4 Flash. First-party token pricing is inherited from `deepseek-v4-flash`: $0.14 input (cache miss) / $0.28 output per million tokens.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek Table 7 reports this as the Max reasoning-effort mode of DeepSeek V4 Flash. First-party token pricing is inherited from `deepseek-v4-flash`: $0.14 input (cache miss) / $0.28 output per million tokens.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek publishes open weights for DeepSeek V4 Flash Base, but first-party API pricing is published for post-trained `deepseek-v4-flash` rather than this exact Base SKU.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek lists `deepseek-v4-pro` at $1.74 input (cache miss) / $3.48 output per million tokens, with cache-hit input at $0.145.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek Table 7 reports this as the High reasoning-effort mode of DeepSeek V4 Pro. First-party token pricing is inherited from `deepseek-v4-pro`: $1.74 input (cache miss) / $3.48 output per million tokens.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek Table 7 reports this as the Max reasoning-effort mode of DeepSeek V4 Pro. First-party token pricing is inherited from `deepseek-v4-pro`: $1.74 input (cache miss) / $3.48 output per million tokens.

items.creator: DeepSeek
items.note: DeepSeek publishes open weights for DeepSeek V4 Pro Base, but first-party API pricing is published for post-trained `deepseek-v4-pro` rather than this exact Base SKU.

items.creator: DeepSeek

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's current first-party docs show Gemini 1.0 Pro is no longer supported, and we did not find a current public token-pricing row for the exact model.

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's Gemini pricing docs list Gemini 1.5 Pro at $1.25 input / $5.00 output per million tokens for prompts up to 128K tokens, rising to $2.50 / $10.00 above 128K.

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's Gemini Developer API pricing page lists Gemini 2.5 Flash Standard pricing at $0.30 input / $2.50 output per million tokens for text, image, and video; Batch pricing is lower.

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's Gemini Developer API pricing page lists Gemini 2.5 Pro Standard pricing at $1.25 input / $10.00 output per million tokens for prompts up to 200K tokens, rising to $2.50 / $15.00 above 200K. Batch and Flex pricing are lower.

items.creator: Google

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's Gemini Developer API pricing page lists Gemini 3 Pro Preview at $2.00 input / $12.00 output per million tokens for prompts up to 200K tokens, rising to $4.00 / $18.00 above 200K.

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google prices `gemini-3-pro-preview`, but we did not find a separate first-party public price row for the exact Gemini 3 Pro Deep Think SKU.

items.creator: Google

items.creator: Google
items.note: Google's Gemini Developer API pricing page lists Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview at $2.00 input / $12.00 output per million tokens for prompts up to 200K tokens, rising to $4.00 / $18.00 above 200K.

items.creator: Google
items.note: Artificial Analysis' May 19, 2026 Gemini 3.5 Flash model page lists Google's API at $1.50 input / $9.00 output per million tokens, with $0.15/M cache-hit pricing.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI's launch materials refer to `grok-beta`, but we did not find a first-party public token-pricing row for the exact `grok-3-beta` SKU.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI's models and pricing page lists grok-3-mini at $0.30 input / $0.50 output per million tokens.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI says Grok 4 is available via API, but the current first-party pricing pages do not expose a clean standalone token-pricing row for the exact `grok-4` alias.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI's current public API pricing pages list Grok 4.20 and Grok 4.1 Fast, but do not publish a current API rate for Grok 4.1. BenchLM treats Grok 4.1 pricing as unavailable until xAI posts an official price.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI's public API pricing pages list grok-4-1-fast at $0.20 input / $0.50 output per million tokens.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: Official xAI docs pricing for grok-4.20-0309-reasoning. xAI's current model docs present this row as Grok 4.20. A non-reasoning variant also exists at the same token price.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI documents Grok 4.20 Multi-agent as a public API model, but the current public pricing pages do not publish a clean standalone token rate for this exact SKU. BenchLM treats pricing as unavailable until xAI posts an explicit list price.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: OpenRouter's live models API lists `x-ai/grok-4.3` at 0.00000125 prompt tokens and 0.0000025 completion tokens, equivalent to $1.25 input / $2.50 output per million tokens, with 1M context and text+image input. OpenRouter's model page also shows a >200K total-token tier at $2.50 input / $5.00 output per million tokens, with cache reads rising from $0.20/M to $0.40/M.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: OpenRouter's May 20, 2026 model page and live models API list `x-ai/grok-build-0.1` with a 256K context window, text+image input, text output, and 0.000001 prompt / 0.000002 completion token pricing, equivalent to $1.00 input / $2.00 output per million tokens. OpenRouter also lists cache reads at $0.20/M.

items.creator: xAI
items.note: xAI's official Grok Code Fast 1 launch post lists $0.20 input / $1.50 output per million tokens, with cached input at $0.02.

items.creator: H Company
items.note: H Company's Holo Models API lists Holo3-122B-A10B at $0.40 input / $3.00 output per million tokens with a 65,536-token context limit, text+image input, a 5-image limit, and paid-tier access.

items.creator: H Company
items.note: Self-hosted open-weight model. H Company's current Holo Models API prices the successor Holo3.1 35B model under model ID holo3-1-35b-a3b; BenchLM has not found current first-party token pricing for this older exact Holo3-35B-A3B row.

items.creator: Poolside
items.note: Poolside's April 28, 2026 Laguna launch post says Laguna M.1 is free to use for a limited time through the Poolside API and OpenRouter. No permanent paid token rate is published yet.

items.note: Mistral's official Mistral Medium 3 model card lists $0.40 input / $2.00 output per million tokens in Mistral AI Studio.

items.creator: Mistral
items.note: Mistral's April 29, 2026 launch post lists Mistral Medium 3.5 API pricing at $1.50 input / $7.50 output per million tokens and describes the 128B dense model as open weights under a modified MIT license.

items.creator: Mistral
items.note: Mistral's official Mistral Small 4 model card lists $0.15 input / $0.60 output per million tokens and describes Mistral Small 4 as a single hybrid model for instruct, reasoning, and coding.

items.creator: Mistral
items.note: BenchLM uses the published Mistral Small 4 Studio price for the reasoning sibling because Mistral describes Small 4 as one hybrid model covering instruct, reasoning, and coding at $0.15 input / $0.60 output per million tokens.

items.creator: ByteDance
items.note: Volcengine publishes first-party CNY pricing for Doubao-Seed-1.6 only: 0-32K input is ¥0.8 input and ¥2 or ¥8 output depending on output-length band; 32-128K is ¥1.2 / ¥16; 128-256K is ¥2.4 / ¥24 per million tokens. BenchLM leaves the USD fields unavailable.

items.creator: ByteDance
items.note: Volcengine publishes first-party CNY pricing for Doubao-Seed-1.6-flash only: 0-32K is ¥0.15 / ¥1.5, 32-128K is ¥0.3 / ¥3, and 128-256K is ¥0.6 / ¥6 per million tokens. BenchLM leaves the USD fields unavailable.

items.creator: ByteDance
items.note: Volcengine publishes first-party CNY pricing for doubao-seed-2.0-lite only: 0-32K is ¥0.6 / ¥3.6, 32-128K is ¥0.9 / ¥5.4, and 128-256K is ¥1.8 / ¥10.8 per million tokens. BenchLM leaves the USD fields unavailable.

items.creator: ByteDance
items.note: Volcengine publishes first-party CNY pricing for doubao-seed-2.0-mini only: 0-32K is ¥0.2 / ¥2, 32-128K is ¥0.4 / ¥4, and 128-256K is ¥0.8 / ¥8 per million tokens. BenchLM leaves the USD fields unavailable.

items.creator: StepFun
items.note: StepFun's official pricing page lists step-3.5-flash at $0.10 input / $0.30 output per million tokens, with a separate cached-input rate.

items.creator: StepFun
items.note: StepFun's official Step 3.7 Flash model card lists input cache-miss pricing at $0.20/M tokens, cache-hit pricing at $0.04/M tokens, and output pricing at $1.15/M tokens.
